Interns will work within the Ethics Office under the Sector of the Director General. The Ethics Office plays a crucial role in ensuring that all WIPO personnel uphold the highest standards of integrity, professionalism, and ethical conduct according to:

  • The WIPO Convention
  • WIPO Staff Regulations and Rules
  • The WIPO Code of Ethics
  • Standards of Conduct for the International Civil Service

The office also provides confidential ethics advice, develops ethics-related policies, conducts awareness campaigns, and protects personnel from retaliation or reprisals.

Internship Location and Duration

Internship Location

  • Geneva, Switzerland

Expected Start Date

  • October 1, 2026

Internship Duration

  • Initial duration of 6 months
  • Renewable up to 12 months maximum

Working Arrangement

  • Full-time
  • 40 hours per week
  • On-site internship at WIPO headquarters

Financial Benefits and Internship Support

Selected interns will receive several benefits during the internship period.

Monthly Stipend

Interns will receive:

  • 2,170 Swiss francs per month

Additional Benefits

The internship package also includes:

  • Medical insurance
  • Annual leave
  • Sick leave benefits
  • Technical supervision and mentorship
  • Professional development within the UN system

This stipend is intended for full-time participation in the internship program.

What Interns Will Do

Interns will support the Ethics Office in legal, policy, communication, and research-related tasks.

Main Responsibilities Include

1. Ethics and Policy Research

  • Conduct research for new or revised ethics policies
  • Analyze ethics-related frameworks within the UN system
  • Support comparative legal and policy studies

2. Legal Research and Fact-Finding

  • Collect information and documentation related to ethics cases
  • Conduct jurisprudence and international civil service law research
  • Assist with legal analysis and policy interpretation

3. Ethics Advisory Support

  • Assist in preparing ethics-related guidance
  • Support the development of confidential advisory materials

4. Communication and Awareness Activities

  • Contribute to ethics outreach campaigns
  • Help prepare educational materials and presentations
  • Assist in awareness-raising initiatives

5. Documentation and Administrative Tasks

  • Draft memoranda, reports, and correspondence
  • Prepare PowerPoint presentations
  • Support updates to Ethics Office web pages and intranet content

6. Additional Tasks

  • Perform other related duties assigned by the Chief Ethics Officer

Eligibility Criteria

WIPO specifies that this internship falls under Category II internships.

Applicants must belong to one of the following categories:

Eligible Applicants Include

  1. Individuals pursuing their first advanced university degree who have completed coursework but not their final thesis
  2. Individuals who already hold one advanced degree and are pursuing another university degree
  3. Individuals who obtained their most recent advanced university degree less than two years before applying

Educational Requirements

Essential Requirement

Applicants must possess:

  • An advanced degree in law (Master’s level preferred)

Preferred academic specializations include:

  • Public international law
  • Organizational ethics
  • Compliance
  • International civil service law

Desirable Qualification

  • Specialization in international civil service law

Preferred Experience

While not mandatory, WIPO values applicants with:

  • Previous experience in international organizations
  • Policy research experience
  • Experience developing communication materials
  • Familiarity with ethics or compliance systems

Language Requirements

Essential

  • Excellent written and spoken English

Desirable

  • Very good knowledge of French

Skills and Competencies Required

WIPO is seeking candidates with strong professional and interpersonal skills.

Key Competencies Include

  • Excellent analytical abilities
  • Strong legal drafting skills
  • Effective written and verbal communication
  • Teamwork and flexibility
  • Integrity and professionalism
  • Sound judgment
  • Confidentiality handling skills
  • Strong organizational capacity
  • Ability to work with sensitive information

Technical Skills

Applicants should also be proficient in:

  • Microsoft Office applications
  • Zoom
  • Digital collaboration tools
  • Online communication systems

Organizational Competencies

Interns are expected to demonstrate:

  • Effective communication
  • Integrity
  • Service orientation
  • Innovation and adaptability
  • Team spirit
  • Respect for diversity
  • Result-oriented performance
  • Continuous learning

Why This Internship Is Important

This WIPO internship is a major opportunity for individuals seeking careers in:

  • International law
  • Human rights
  • Ethics and compliance
  • UN agencies
  • International governance
  • Public policy
  • Diplomacy
  • Global institutions

Interns will gain first-hand exposure to how ethics systems function inside a major international organization and develop practical experience that can significantly strengthen future career prospects.

How to Apply for the WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026

Applicants must submit the following documents:

Required Documents

  • Motivation letter
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Submission Instructions

Applications should be sent by email to:

  • erecruit@wipo.int

Required Subject Line

  • Ethics Internship position 2026

Important Note

For LinkedIn Easy Apply submissions:

  • Combine both the CV and cover letter into one PDF file

Applications without both documents will not be considered.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 is:

June 19, 2026

Candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Applicants can learn more about the internship program on here

Discover more internship opportunities on OFY here

The post WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland | Paid United Nations Internship for Law and International Relations Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Young writers, dreamers, storytellers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to participate in the Social Fiction Design Competition 2026, a global creative writing competition that challenges participants to imagine a better, fairer, and more sustainable world for young people in the year 2050. Organized by Yunus Center and powered through YY Ventures, this […]

The post Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young writers, dreamers, storytellers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to participate in the Social Fiction Design Competition 2026, a global creative writing competition that challenges participants to imagine a better, fairer, and more sustainable world for young people in the year 2050.

Organized by Yunus Center and powered through YY Ventures, this international initiative encourages people everywhere to use the power of imagination and storytelling to envision solutions to today’s social and environmental challenges.

The competition is open globally with no age restrictions and no geographical limitations, making it one of the most inclusive international creative writing opportunities currently available.

Participants stand a chance to win a grand prize of USD 10,000 while contributing to conversations about the future of humanity, sustainability, innovation, and social transformation.

What is the Social Fiction Design Competition?

The Social Fiction Design Competition is an international writing competition focused on “Social Fiction” — a form of imaginative storytelling similar to science fiction, but centered on social innovation and human progress rather than futuristic technology alone.

The competition invites participants to imagine:

  • What the world could look like in 2050
  • How young people may live in the future
  • How societies can overcome inequality and injustice
  • What sustainable communities might become
  • How governments, businesses, and communities can work together for positive change

The goal is to inspire new ways of thinking about social innovation and encourage people to imagine the world they truly want to build.

According to Nobel Peace Laureate Muhammad Yunus:

“If we do not imagine, it will not be done. If we imagine, someday it will happen.”

This powerful idea forms the foundation of the competition.

What is Social Fiction?

Social Fiction is a storytelling approach that imagines a future society shaped by positive social transformation.

Unlike traditional science fiction, which often focuses heavily on futuristic machines or scientific advancement, Social Fiction focuses on:

  • Human-centered innovation
  • Social justice
  • Sustainability
  • Equality
  • Community transformation
  • Ethical development
  • Inclusive economies
  • Environmental healing

The concept encourages writers to creatively explore solutions to real-world problems and imagine societies where communities thrive.

The organizers believe that imagination can inspire real-world innovation and policy change.

Competition Theme: The Life of a Young Person in 2050

Participants are asked to imagine and describe:

“How do you wish to see the life of a young person in the year 2050?”

Writers are encouraged to take readers on a journey into a future world that is free from the social and environmental injustices affecting communities today.

The competition emphasizes optimism, innovation, and bold thinking.

Applicants should imagine a future where systems, institutions, and communities have evolved to create better opportunities and improved quality of life for younger generations.

Writing Requirements

Participants must submit an original Social Fiction piece between:

  • Minimum: 1,000 words
  • Maximum: 5,000 words

The writing should creatively explore a fictional world in 2050 while addressing one or more major social or environmental issues.

Suggested Focus Areas

Participants may choose to focus on one or several of the following areas:

  • Climate Change
  • Agriculture
  • Technology and Innovation
  • Micro-credit
  • Transport
  • Literature
  • Music
  • Waste Management
  • Circular Economy
  • Tourism
  • WASH (Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene)
  • Employment
  • Sports
  • Health and Well-being
  • Community Development
  • Sustainability
  • Social Equity

The organizers encourage participants to think broadly and creatively about how these sectors could evolve positively by 2050.

What Should Your Story Include?

The competition encourages writers to explain how different stakeholders contribute to building a better future.

Stories may include the role of:

  • Governments
  • Educational institutions
  • Corporate organizations
  • Local businesses
  • Community leaders
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Youth movements
  • Civil society organizations

Participants should demonstrate how collaboration and innovation help shape a more just and sustainable society.

Why This Competition Matters

The Social Fiction Design Competition is not just a writing contest. It is a platform for global imagination and social innovation.

At a time when the world faces challenges such as:

  • Climate change
  • Rising inequality
  • Unemployment
  • Environmental degradation
  • Mental health concerns
  • Economic instability
  • Social exclusion

The competition seeks bold visions of hope and possibility.

It encourages people to move beyond criticism and begin imagining actionable futures worth building.

Prize Opportunity: Win USD 10,000

One of the major attractions of the competition is the opportunity to win:

USD 10,000

The prize recognizes outstanding creativity, originality, optimism, and social imagination.

Beyond financial rewards, participants gain international exposure and become part of a global conversation on innovation and sustainable futures.

Who Can Participate?

One of the strongest features of the Social Fiction Design Competition is its accessibility.

There are:

  • No age restrictions
  • No geographical limitations

This means anyone from anywhere in the world can participate.

Students, writers, professionals, activists, researchers, entrepreneurs, and aspiring storytellers are all encouraged to apply.

Can You Apply More Than Once?

According to the competition FAQ:

  • Multiple applications are not permitted.

Applicants are encouraged to focus on submitting one strong and well-developed Social Fiction piece.

Is There an Application Fee?

The competition states that there is:

  • No application fee

Participation is completely free.

Can Teams Apply?

The FAQ section indicates that applicants should review the application guidelines regarding team participation.

Participants are encouraged to verify current submission rules through the official competition platform.

Previous Winners and Finalists

The competition has already attracted talented participants from different countries around the world.

Previous Winner

  • Amit Kumar Mishra – Nepal

Previous Finalists

  • Neha Sabu – India
  • Wan Faizal Mohd – Malaysia
  • Anne Julienne Barcelona – United States
  • Tyfur Rahman – Bangladesh

The diversity of past finalists demonstrates the truly international nature of the competition.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the Social Fiction Design Competition can help writers and changemakers:

  • Showcase creativity globally
  • Build visibility as a thought leader
  • Contribute to social innovation conversations
  • Inspire real-world solutions
  • Develop storytelling and writing skills
  • Gain recognition for visionary ideas
  • Connect imagination with impact

For young innovators, students, activists, and creative thinkers, this is a unique opportunity to influence future thinking through storytelling.

How to Apply

Applicants are required to:

  1. Write an original Social Fiction piece
  2. Ensure the submission falls within 1,000–5,000 words
  3. Complete the online application form
  4. Submit the story through the official competition platform

For inquiries:

  • Email: info@yy.ventures
  • WhatsApp: +880 1648-397420

Apply Here 

For more information on this competition visit here 

Discover more opportunities on OFY here 

The post Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
